name: Veriforce Contractor & Supply Chain Safety API
what_it_does: Veriforce REST API for contractor and supply chain safety management platform. Enables AI agents to manage contractor prequalification status and safety program compliance verification, handle worker qualification and training record retrieval for site access decisions, access insurance certificate and OSHA recordable incident rate (TRIR, LTIR) data for contractor risk assessment, retrieve safety audit and inspection finding data for contractor performance monitoring, manage operator qualification (OQ) program compliance for pipeline and utility contractors, handle contractor onboarding workflow and document collection automation, access site-specific safety orientation completion tracking, retrieve regulatory compliance status across OSHA, DOT, and EPA requirements, manage contractor tiering and performance scoring for supply chain risk, and integrate contractor safety data with ERP, procurement, and HSE management systems.
use_cases: Verifying contractor prequalification from procurement agents
Checking worker qualifications from site access control agents
Monitoring contractor safety metrics from HSE management agents
Automating contractor onboarding from supply chain agents
not_for: Non-industrial contractor management without safety prequalification needs
Simple vendor management without worker safety and qualification requirements
Consumer markets without enterprise contractor supply chain context
best_when: An energy company, utility, or industrial operator using Veriforce wants AI agents to automate contractor prequalification verification, worker qualification tracking, insurance verification, and safety performance monitoring across the contractor supply chain.
avoid_when: SAFETY RISK: Contractor prequalification data reflects point-in-time compliance — automated site access approval based on prequalification status alone without current safety record verification creates incidents. TRIR/LTIR data can lag actual incidents; use as one input in broader contractor risk assessment. Operator qualification (OQ) compliance for pipeline work has strict DOT requirements; automated OQ verification errors create regulatory violations.

auth.notes: Veriforce uses OAuth 2.0 and API key authentication. Per-operator credentials. Webhooks for contractor status and qualification events. REST API with JSON. The Woodlands, Texas HQ. Formerly PEC Safety + Contractor Compliance Management (CCM) merger. Strong in pipeline, oil & gas, and utility contractor management. Operator Qualification (OQ) compliance for DOT-regulated pipelines. Competes with ISNetworld and Avetta for contractor safety prequalification.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — annual enterprise subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription; contractor and worker volume pricing
pricing.notes: The Woodlands, Texas. Founded via PEC Safety + CCM merger. 700+ operator clients. 80,000+ contractor companies. 3M+ worker records. Strong in energy (pipeline, utilities, oil & gas). Operator Qualification (OQ) compliance is core differentiator vs. ISNetworld. Veriforce Connect is data sharing platform. Competes with ISNetworld (Houston TX) and Avetta (Lehi UT) for contractor management market.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; documented per client

known_agent_gotchas: SAFETY RISK: Prequalification status is point-in-time; contractor may have had safety incidents after prequalification; validate current incident rates before site access decisions
Operator Qualification (OQ) compliance — DOT 49 CFR Part 192/195 requires individual worker OQ records for pipeline tasks; automated OQ verification must match task type to qualified task codes
TRIR/LTIR lag — incident rate data may be 6-12 months behind due to OSHA reporting cycles; don't rely on TRIR for real-time safety status

Contractor vs. worker level data — prequalification is at contractor company level; individual worker qualification and training are separate records; don't conflate company-level and worker-level status
Insurance certificate recency — COI data expires and must be re-verified; automated access decisions based on cached insurance data without re-verification creates liability exposure
Pipeline safety regulatory requirements — DOT-regulated pipeline work requires specific OQ task codes; verify correct task code mapping for automated compliance checks
agent_readiness.security_notes: Contractor safety and qualification data. SOC2. OAuth2/API key. US/CA. Worker qualification, insurance, and safety incident data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Contractor safety management. SOC2. OAuth2. US. Worker qualification and contractor safety data.
